title: Caramelized Venezuelan Onion Salad
description: A floral, light salad that turns humble onions into caramelized jewels, accented with thyme and balsamic for depth and a touch of maple sweetness. This plantbased dish is both adventurous and comforting, ideal for a quick lunch or a vibrant side.
instructions: Thinly slice the red onions into even, fine rings for maximum surface area.
Heat the olive oil and coconut oil in a large skillet over medium heat until shimmering but not smoking.
Add the onions, stirring to coat them in the oils; cook for 6–8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they begin to soften.
Pour in the maple syrup and balsamic vinegar, stirring to combine and coat the onions.
Continue cooking, stirring frequently, until the onions are deep golden brown and caramelized, about 15–20 minutes.
Season with sea salt, black pepper, and fresh thyme leaves, stirring to distribute the flavors.
Remove the skillet from heat and allow the onions to cool for 5 minutes; the caramelization will deepen as they rest.
Transfer the caramelized onions to a serving bowl, sprinkle with chopped cilantro, and toss gently to coat.
Serve immediately as a bright, aromatic side dish or atop a bed of mixed greens for a full meal.
